name: Kentucky Bourbon Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Marinate raisins in 1 cup of the bourbon for at least 1 hour, up to 12 hours; drain and reserve both raisins and bourbon.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
3. Butter and flour a 10-inch Bundt cake pan or other tube-cake pan.
4. Beat together butter and sugar until light and fluffy using an electric mixer. Start at slow speed, gradually increasing to moderate. If allowed about 15 minutes, the mixture will be very pale and look like whipped cream, adding lightness to the cake.
5. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
6. In another bowl, sift together 4 cups flour, baking powder, soda, and salt.
7. Combine reserved bourbon with lemon zest and juice.
8. Alternate adding dry ingredients and bourbon mixture to the butter mixture.
9. Toss remaining flour with drained raisins and pecans; fold into batter.
10. Bake for about 1 hour and 30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
11. Cool for about 10 minutes in the pan before turning onto a rack.
12. Brush remaining bourbon over the cake while still warm.
13. For long storage, wrap the cake in moistened cheesecloth, then plastic or foil to keep it airtight. It can be served after a day or two, but is best if allowed to age.
